episode_summary: Mary Roach joins Dan, James, and Andy for a fact-packed episode covering early blood banks that hid staff behind partition walls, moss forensics solving crimes, the first breast implants tested on a dog, and New Mexico’s unique official state smell. Along the way they explore forensics, medical implants, and the quirky world of U.S. state symbols and smells.

facts.fact: At the early blood banks, we're talking around 1915 or so, the staff sat hidden behind a wall with holes cut every few feet. Donors would come in, sit down, stick their arm through a hole, and thereby be spared the sight of needles and blood.
Okay, my fact this week is that moss can solve crimes but it's woefully underappreciated.
My fact this week is that the first recipient of breast implants had to have them removed after she chewed through the stitches.
My fact is, there is only one American state with an official state smell.
